author: kaper
description: Edit and preview Kaper recipes inline as structured blocks. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: neex
manifest.description: Edit and preview Kaper recipes inline as structured blocks.
manifest.name: Kaper

name: Kaper

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Recipes can now be cooked without ever leaving Obsidian.

### Added

- **In-app Cook Mode.** Step through a recipe one step at a time, right inside Obsidian — a full-screen sheet on mobile, a dockable side panel on desktop — with a progress bar and ← / → arrow-key navigation. Replaces the previous behaviour of opening kaper.me in the browser.
- **Reading-mode rendering.** Recipes now render as a read-only preview in Reading view, not just Live Preview, with a "Start Cooking" action there too.
- **"View on Kaper" link.** The recipe preview links out to the full Kaper web app at kaper.me, for anyone who still wants the browser experience.
- Recipe images now save under a per-recipe `_assets/{recipeId}/` folder, matching the Kaper web app's layout. Existing flat-named images from earlier versions still resolve correctly.

### Changed

- Internal: consolidated duplicated icon/callout/image-handling code across the preview and Cook mode into shared components; recipe "is this empty / does it have steps" logic is now a single set of helpers instead of being re-derived per call site.
- Internal: the Kaper tab strip now follows Obsidian's `--tab-*` theme variables, so themes that restyle tabs apply to Kaper's Preview/Form tabs too.
- Internal: the recipe parser's YAML round-trip now goes through Obsidian's own `parseYaml`/`stringifyYaml` instead of a bundled `js-yaml` dependency.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/nikoneex/obsidian-kaper/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

- Timer and animation-frame calls now go through `window` (`window.setTimeout`, `window.requestAnimationFrame`) for correct behaviour in popped-out windows.

### Changed

- Internal: added an ESLint guardrail (`eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` + `typescript-eslint`) that reproduces the Obsidian plugin-review checks and runs in CI, plus type-safety cleanups across the recipe-id, settings, and form code paths.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/nikoneex/obsidian-kaper/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

- `minAppVersion` raised to `1.4.4` to match the `app.fileManager.processFrontMatter` API the plugin relies on (added in Obsidian 1.4.4). The previous `1.4.0` floor would let the plugin install on app versions where recipe-id stamping throws.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Thanks to [@bluelephant825](https://github.com/bluelephant825), whose [PR #4](https://github.com/nikoneex/obsidian-kaper/pull/4) surfaced the image and prep-time features and the time-field bug below. These were reimplemented to fit the plugin's recipe format and code style.

### Added

- Cover images and per-step images now render in the recipe preview — resolved from vault paths, `_assets/` uploads, or remote URLs, and hidden gracefully when an image can't be loaded. (#4)
- Cover image upload in the recipe form's Basics section.
- Mobile support (read-only): recipes render on Obsidian mobile. Editing — the form editor, Cook mode, and recipe creation/conversion — stays desktop-only for now while the form is redesigned for small screens.
- Prep time now appears alongside cook time in the preview. (#4)

### Fixed

- Form time fields now show the recipe's saved prep/cook time on open instead of starting blank. (#4)

### Changed

- Recipe schema aligned with the Kaper web app: the legacy `equipment` and app-managed `_app` keys are no longer part of the format and are shed on save. The format doc now documents the canonical stable `kaper: r_<id>` marker.
- Step tip and warning callouts restyled to match the Kaper web app; the warning label is now "Heads up".
- README: documented that a remote `coverImage`/step image URL is fetched directly by Obsidian from a host outside Kaper's control, and added a Contributing section.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Pairs with the new file format that kaper.me writes after opening a vault — recipes now carry a stable id in frontmatter (`kaper: r_<nanoid>`) so favorites and cook history stick to the recipe across renames, moves, and copies.

### What changed

- **Recognises the new `kaper: r_<id>` frontmatter.** Recipes stamped by kaper.me now render correctly in Obsidian. Existing files with `kaper: true` keep working — both shapes are accepted as recipe markers.
- **Preserves the recipe id through edits.** Editing a recipe in the form (or running **Kaper: Convert current note to recipe** on a file that already has an id) won't clobber the id. Your favorites stay attached.
- **Tolerates recipes without a title.** A recipe written without a `title:` key no longer errors out — it just renders with an empty title. Fill it in from the form.
- **Round-trips unknown YAML keys at the bottom of the block.** If a future kaper version (or you, by hand) adds an `_app:` section, the plugin preserves it on save instead of dropping it.
- **Tag input is now chip-based.** Type a tag, press enter, get a chip. Backspace removes the last one. Replaces the comma-separated text field.
- **Empty-state nudge in the preview.** Newly-created recipes show a friendlier "add ingredients or steps" prompt instead of a blank preview.
- **Cleaner internals for the Community Plugin review.** Popout-window safe (`activeDocument`/`activeWindow`), no leaked listeners on plugin reload, no `:has()` selectors in CSS, no postinstall-blocked installs under pnpm.

### Installation

Drop `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/kaper/` folder, then Settings → Community plugins → enable **Kaper**.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Patch release.

  - Marked as `isDesktopOnly: true` until Obsidian Mobile compatibility is
  verified end-to-end. Code paths are platform-agnostic, but the safe default for
  v1 is desktop-only; mobile will return in a follow-up.
  - Internal: `normalizePath()` for vault path construction, format spec doc
  cleanup.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Edit and preview Kaper recipes inline as structured blocks in Obsidian's Live Preview. 
Recipes round-trip cleanly with [kaper.me](https://kaper.me) — open the same vault in either app.

  ## What's included

  - **Live Preview rendering** — markdown files with a `kaper: true` frontmatter
  and a ` ```kaper ` code block render the recipe form/preview in place.
  - **Form editor** — title, servings, difficulty, prep/cook time, tags,
  ingredient groups (drag-to-reorder), steps (with notes, tips, warnings,
  technique, duration).
  - **Preview** — adapts to your Obsidian theme and accent color.
  - **Cook Mode** — one click jumps to kaper.me for the full hands-free cooking
  experience.
  - **Create / Convert commands** — ribbon button and command palette entries.

  ## Manual installation (pre-Community-Plugin)

  1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below.
  2. Copy them into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/kaper/`.
  3. In Obsidian: Settings → Community plugins → enable **Kaper**.

  See the [README](https://github.com/nikoneex/obsidian-kaper#readme) for full docs.
